articles.headline: Government Launches National Green Hydrogen Mission - Phase II, Targets Export Hub Status
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government unveils the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and fostering an export-oriented ecosystem.
• New incentives include production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production, along with land allocation and infrastructure development.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• Focus areas include research and development in cost-reduction technologies, skill development, and strategic international partnerships for market access.
• This phase is crucial for India's energy security, decarbonization goals, and creating green jobs across the value chain.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Phase II targets India as a global green hydrogen export hub by 2030.
PLI scheme for electrolyzers and green hydrogen production is a key component.
Aims to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve climate targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic and environmental significance of India's Green Hydrogen Mission.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global green hydrogen hub.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in accelerating the green hydrogen economy.
Examine the potential impact on India's energy security and geopolitical standing.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with renewable energy sources like solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Former MP and Rajasthan MLA Nand Lal Meena Passes Away at 79
articles.explainer:
• Veteran politician Nand Lal Meena, who served as a Member of Parliament and multiple terms as a Member of Legislative Assembly in Rajasthan, passed away at the age of 79.
• Meena represented the Salumber constituency in the Lok Sabha from 1989 to 1991.
• He was a prominent figure in Rajasthan politics, having been elected as an MLA from the Pratapgarh constituency for several terms between 1998 and 2013.
• Known for his focus on tribal welfare and regional development, he contributed significantly to public life in his home state.
• His demise marks the end of an era for a dedicated public servant from Rajasthan.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Nand Lal Meena was a former MP (1989-1991) from Salumber.
Served as Rajasthan MLA (1998-2013) from Pratapgarh.
Associated with tribal welfare in Rajasthan.
articles.imageDescription: Black and white photo of a senior Indian politician, Nand Lal Meena.
articles.trivia: The first Lok Sabha elections in independent India were held from 1951-52.

articles.headline: India Gains Momentum for UNSC Reform with Strong African Union Support
articles.explainer:
• India's long-standing demand for permanent membership in a reformed UN Security Council (UNSC) receives significant backing from the African Union (AU).
• During the ongoing UN General Assembly session, several AU member states reiterated their support for expanding the UNSC to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
• India has consistently advocated for comprehensive UNSC reforms, emphasizing equitable representation for developing nations, including from Africa and Latin America.
• This renewed support comes amidst growing calls for a more inclusive and effective global governance architecture capable of addressing 21st-century challenges.
• The G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) continue to coordinate efforts to push for reforms.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: India is a member of the G4 group advocating for UNSC reform.
African Union has expressed support for expanding UNSC permanent membership.
UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
mainsPoints: Analyze the arguments for and against India's permanent membership in the UNSC.
Discuss the significance of African Union's support for India's UNSC aspirations.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving comprehensive UN Security Council reforms.
Examine how a reformed UNSC could better address global challenges.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations building in New York with flags of member countries, symbolizing international diplomacy.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council held its first session on January 17, 1946.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework for Sustainable Urban Development Emphasizes Green Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveils a comprehensive policy framework aimed at promoting sustainable urban development across Indian cities.
• Key focus areas include integrating green infrastructure, promoting waste-to-energy projects, and enhancing urban resilience to climate change.
• The policy mandates the adoption of green building codes, increased public transport usage, and decentralized waste management systems.
• It also emphasizes citizen participation in urban planning and leveraging technology for smart governance.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitments under the Paris Agreement and Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: New policy framework by Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Focus on green infrastructure and waste-to-energy in urban areas.
Aims to achieve SDG 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities.
Promotes green building codes and decentralized waste management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of rapid urbanization in India and how sustainable urban development policies can address them.
Evaluate the role of green infrastructure and waste-to-energy projects in achieving urban sustainability.
Critically analyze the effectiveness of current urban planning models in India and suggest improvements.
Examine the potential of citizen participation and technology in fostering smart and sustainable cities.
articles.imageDescription: A modern cityscape with green spaces, solar panels on rooftops, and efficient public transport, depicting sustainable urban living.
articles.trivia: Around 35% of India's population lives in urban areas, a number projected to increase significantly by 2050.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Crucial Component for Gaganyaan Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful testing of a critical component for the Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission.
• This test involved the integrated performance validation of the crew escape system's propulsion elements under simulated flight conditions.
• The successful validation brings ISRO closer to its ambitious goal of sending Indian astronauts into space aboard an indigenous spacecraft.
• Future tests will focus on full-scale abort missions and further integration of various modules.
• The Gaganyaan mission aims to demonstrate India's capability for human spaceflight by launching a crew of three into a low earth orbit for a mission duration of three days.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
ISRO is the agency responsible for Gaganyaan.
Crew escape system is a vital safety component for manned missions.
Mission aims to send 3 astronauts to LEO for 3 days.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance and scientific benefits of India's Gaganyaan mission.
Analyze the technological challenges and advancements required for human spaceflight missions.
Evaluate the potential spin-off technologies and economic benefits from space missions like Gaganyaan.
Examine India's growing role in global space exploration and cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: An artist's impression of the Gaganyaan spacecraft module with the Indian flag, orbiting Earth.
articles.trivia: India's first astronaut, Rakesh Sharma, flew aboard a Soviet Soyuz T-11 mission in 1984.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling clarified that certain provisions, particularly those concerning the balance between individual privacy and national security/public order, are permissible under reasonable restrictions.
• While acknowledging concerns raised by petitioners regarding potential surveillance, the Court emphasized the necessity of a robust legal framework for data protection in the digital age.
• The judgment is expected to provide much-needed clarity for technology companies and data fiduciaries operating in India.
• This decision reinforces India's commitment to protecting citizen data while also allowing the state to maintain security and public order, setting a precedent for future digital governance.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Right to Privacy
Cybersecurity
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act, 2023 is India's primary data protection law.
Supreme Court decision relates to constitutional validity.
Balances privacy with national security/public order.
Puttaswamy judgment established Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the DPDP Act for individual privacy and state surveillance.
Discuss the challenges in balancing the right to privacy with concerns of national security and public order in the digital age.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the DPDP Act in protecting citizen data and fostering a responsible digital economy.
Examine the global trends in data protection laws and India's position within this landscape.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade, representing legal authority and justice.
articles.trivia: Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) and Anr. vs Union Of India And Ors. (2017) is the landmark case that declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Digital Governance

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision comes amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and fluctuating commodity prices, which pose risks to domestic inflation.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to maintaining price stability while supporting sustainable economic growth.
• Analysts suggest that the RBI is adopting a cautious 'wait and watch' approach, assessing the impact of previous rate hikes and global developments before further adjustments.
• The central bank reiterated its focus on liquidity management to ensure financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines policy rates in India.
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability.
Current inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain status quo on policy rates.
Analyze the impact of global economic headwinds on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the RBI's current monetary policy stance in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Examine the challenges faced by the MPC in achieving its dual mandate.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Maritime Security Cooperation in Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• India has significantly enhanced its maritime security cooperation with key partners in the Indo-Pacific region through a series of multilateral exercises and bilateral agreements.
• The focus is on strengthening interoperability, information sharing, and collective response capabilities to address traditional and non-traditional security threats.
• Recent joint naval exercises with QUAD partners (US, Japan, Australia) and ASEAN countries underscore India's commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• This strategic push aims to counter growing challenges such as piracy, illegal fishing, and maritime terrorism, while also ensuring freedom of navigation.
• India's vision for SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) guides its engagements, emphasizing capacity building and collaborative security frameworks.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: SAGAR doctrine relates to India's vision for security and growth in the Indian Ocean Region.
QUAD members are India, USA, Japan, Australia.
Malabar exercise involves QUAD nations.
Indo-Pacific is a key geopolitical region for India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving maritime security challenges in the Indo-Pacific region and India's strategic response.
Analyze the significance of India's partnerships with QUAD and ASEAN countries in enhancing regional maritime security.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the SAGAR doctrine in promoting a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Examine the implications of increased maritime cooperation for India's geopolitical influence and economic interests.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Navy warships sailing in formation with other international naval vessels during a joint exercise in the open sea.
articles.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's five oceans.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Reach, Integrates More Healthcare Providers
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has announced a significant expansion, bringing a greater number of public and private healthcare providers under its digital health ecosystem.
• This expansion aims to create a seamless online platform for patients to access their health records, consult doctors, and avail other services.
• The integration includes more hospitals, clinics, diagnostic labs, and pharmacies, enhancing the interoperability of health data across the country.
• A key feature is the emphasis on creating unique Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA) for citizens, acting as a digital repository for their medical history.
• The mission is pivotal in achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare delivery efficiency through digital means, aligning with the National Health Policy 2017.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem in India.
ABHA is a unique health ID for citizens.
National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
Focus on integrating public and private healthcare providers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in transforming India's healthcare landscape.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a nationwide digital health ecosystem, particularly concerning data privacy and digital literacy.
Evaluate how ABDM can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare access in rural areas.
Examine the ethical considerations and safeguards required for a robust digital health platform.
articles.imageDescription: A digital interface displaying patient health records and a doctor interacting with a tablet, symbolizing digital healthcare.
articles.trivia: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Support Prices for Rabi Crops, Focus on Farmer Income
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced the Minimum Support Prices (MSPs) for the upcoming Rabi marketing season, with significant hikes for key crops.
• The move is aimed at ensuring remunerative prices for farmers, encouraging crop diversification, and bolstering agricultural productivity.
• Wheat, barley, gram, masur (lentil), rapeseed & mustard, and safflower are among the crops for which MSPs have been increased.
• The government highlighted its commitment to implementing the Swaminathan Committee recommendations, ensuring MSPs are at least 1.5 times the cost of production.
• This policy decision is expected to have a positive impact on farmer incomes and address concerns regarding agricultural distress, while also managing food inflation.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security.
keyTerms: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsPoints: MSP is announced by the government for certain crops to protect farmers from price fluctuations.
Rabi crops are sown in winter and harvested in spring (e.g., wheat, barley, gram).
CACP recommends MSPs.
Swaminathan Committee recommended MSP at 1.5 times the cost of production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and implications of the government's MSP policy for Rabi crops on farmer welfare and agricultural economy.
Analyze the challenges associated with the implementation of MSP, including procurement, storage, and market distortions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of MSP in ensuring remunerative prices and promoting crop diversification.
Examine alternative mechanisms to support farmer income and address agricultural distress in India.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a lush green wheat field during the Rabi season, symbolizing agricultural productivity.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of wheat, after China.

articles.headline: India-Australia Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Critical Minerals and Green Energy
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Australia Strategic Dialogue concluded with a strong emphasis on enhancing cooperation in critical minerals and green energy technologies.
• Both nations committed to developing resilient supply chains for critical minerals essential for advanced manufacturing and renewable energy sectors.
• Discussions also covered joint research and development initiatives in solar, hydrogen, and battery technologies, leveraging their respective strengths.
• The dialogue reinforced the comprehensive strategic partnership, aiming to diversify trade and investment beyond traditional sectors.
• This collaboration is vital for both countries' energy transition goals and for securing key resources in a geopolitically volatile world.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Australia is rich in critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements.
India-Australia relations elevated to Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2020.
Critical minerals are essential for electric vehicles, electronics, and renewable energy.
Both countries are part of the QUAD grouping.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth and energy transition.
Analyze how India-Australia cooperation in critical minerals and green energy can bolster supply chain resilience and reduce global dependencies.
Evaluate the broader implications of this partnership for the Indo-Pacific region and global energy security.
Examine the potential for joint R&D and investment in advanced energy technologies between the two nations.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Australia against a backdrop of wind turbines and mining operations, symbolizing energy and resources collaboration.
articles.trivia: Australia is the world's largest producer of lithium, a key component in EV batteries.

articles.headline: India Records Significant Solar Energy Capacity Addition in Q3 2025
articles.explainer:
• India has achieved a new milestone in renewable energy, adding a record amount of solar energy capacity in the third quarter of 2025.
• This surge is attributed to supportive government policies, declining solar panel costs, and increased private sector investment.
• The aggressive expansion is crucial for India to meet its ambitious target of 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• States like Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Karnataka continue to lead in solar power generation, attracting major domestic and international players.
• The rapid growth in solar energy contributes significantly to reducing India's carbon footprint and enhancing energy independence.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: India's target is 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Solar energy is a major component of India's renewable energy mix.
Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Karnataka are leading states in solar power.
International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to India's rapid growth in solar energy capacity.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating large-scale solar power into India's national grid.
Evaluate the role of solar energy in achieving India's climate targets and enhancing energy security.
Examine the socio-economic benefits of expanding renewable energy infrastructure, including job creation and rural electrification.
articles.imageDescription: Vast solar panel arrays stretching across a desert landscape under a clear sky, generating clean energy.
articles.trivia: Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the world's largest solar power plants.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Report Highlights Successes in Cooperative Federalism for Flagship Schemes
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by NITI Aayog showcased several successful instances of cooperative federalism in action, particularly in the implementation of national flagship schemes.
• The report analyzed how collaborative efforts between the Centre and various states led to efficient delivery of services and achievement of development targets.
• Case studies included Swachh Bharat Mission, Ayushman Bharat, and Jal Jeevan Mission, highlighting best practices and innovative approaches adopted by states.
• NITI Aayog emphasized its role as a facilitator and think tank, promoting policy dialogue and knowledge sharing between different levels of government.
• This reinforces the importance of a 'Team India' approach to national development, where states are active partners in policy formulation and implementation.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
NITI Aayog
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015.
NITI Aayog acts as a think tank and promotes cooperative federalism.
Swachh Bharat Mission, Ayushman Bharat, Jal Jeevan Mission are flagship schemes.
Cooperative federalism emphasizes collaboration between Centre and States.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of cooperative federalism and its significance in India's governance structure.
Analyze the role of NITI Aayog in fostering cooperative federalism and improving policy implementation.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for Centre-state collaboration in achieving national development goals.
Examine how successful implementation of flagship schemes can be attributed to cooperative federalism.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected gears, symbolizing the collaborative functioning of central and state governments.
articles.trivia: The term 'cooperative federalism' was first used in India by Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru.

articles.headline: UNESCO Recognizes Indian Cultural Landscape as New World Heritage Site
articles.explainer:
• UNESCO has officially inscribed a significant Indian cultural landscape onto its prestigious World Heritage List, marking a major recognition of India's rich heritage.
• The newly recognized site encompasses a unique blend of natural beauty, historical monuments, and living cultural traditions, reflecting centuries of human-environment interaction.
• This designation is expected to boost cultural tourism, create local employment opportunities, and enhance international awareness of India's diverse heritage.
• The government and local communities have committed to implementing robust conservation and management plans to preserve the site's outstanding universal value.
• India now boasts an even greater number of UNESCO World Heritage Sites, solidifying its position as a global cultural destination.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO World Heritage Sites are designated for their outstanding universal value.
Cultural landscapes are a category of World Heritage Sites.
India has numerous UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
UNESCO is a specialized agency of the United Nations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO World Heritage status for the conservation and promotion of India's cultural and natural heritage.
Analyze the challenges involved in managing and preserving cultural landscapes while balancing tourism and local community needs.
Evaluate the economic and social benefits that accrue to local communities from a World Heritage designation.
Examine how such recognition contributes to India's soft power and global cultural diplomacy.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a scenic Indian landscape featuring ancient ruins, traditional settlements, and natural formations.
articles.trivia: The first site to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List was the Galapagos Islands in 1978.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Art & Culture
prelimsSyllabus.topic: UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India

articles.headline: DRDO Successfully Tests Advanced Indigenous Drone System
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) announced the successful flight testing of a new advanced indigenous drone system.
• This Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) is designed for enhanced surveillance, reconnaissance, and logistics support capabilities for the Indian armed forces.
• The test demonstrated its long endurance, advanced navigation systems, and ability to operate in diverse terrains and weather conditions.
• This development is a significant step towards 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) in defense technology, reducing reliance on foreign imports.
• The drone system is expected to augment India's border security and tactical operational capabilities significantly.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Defence Technology
prelimsPoints: DRDO is India's premier defense research agency.
UAVs are increasingly used for surveillance and logistics.
Atmanirbhar Bharat aims for self-reliance in defense manufacturing.
Drones are crucial for modern warfare and border management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous drone technology for India's defense capabilities and national security.
Analyze the role of DRDO in achieving self-reliance in critical defense technologies.
Evaluate the ethical and operational challenges associated with the increasing use of UAVs in military operations.
Examine how advanced drone systems can enhance border management and counter-insurgency operations.
articles.imageDescription: A sleek, modern drone flying over a mountainous border region, performing surveillance.
articles.trivia: The first military drone, the 'Ruston Proctor Aerial Target,' was developed during World War I.

articles.headline: New Initiative Launched to Boost Climate-Resilient Agriculture Practices
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, in collaboration with ICAR, launched a new national initiative to promote climate-resilient agriculture.
• The program focuses on developing and disseminating climate-smart farming practices, drought-resistant crop varieties, and efficient water management techniques.
• It aims to help Indian farmers adapt to the adverse impacts of climate change, such as erratic rainfall, extreme temperatures, and increased pest outbreaks.
• Training and capacity building for farmers, along with financial incentives for adopting sustainable practices, are key components of the initiative.
• This move is crucial for ensuring food security and sustaining agricultural livelihoods in the face of a changing climate.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: ICAR is the apex body for coordinating, guiding and managing research and education in agriculture.
Climate-resilient agriculture aims to adapt to and mitigate climate change impacts.
Focus on drought-resistant varieties and efficient water use.
Crucial for India's food security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and the need for climate-resilient practices.
Analyze the components of the new initiative for climate-resilient agriculture and its potential impact on farmer livelihoods.
Evaluate the role of research and technology in developing climate-smart farming solutions.
Examine how government policies and farmer participation can accelerate the adoption of sustainable agricultural practices.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting healthy, green crops in a field, with advanced irrigation systems, demonstrating climate-resilient farming.
articles.trivia: India is one of the most climate-vulnerable countries, with agriculture being particularly susceptible to climate change impacts.

articles.headline: India Continues to Champion Digital Public Infrastructure at G20 Platforms
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to promoting Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) as a key enabler for inclusive growth and sustainable development at recent G20 meetings.
• Building on its successful domestic models like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, India advocated for global cooperation in developing open, interoperable, and secure DPIs.
• The focus was on leveraging DPI for financial inclusion, digital payments, identity verification, and efficient public service delivery in developing nations.
• Several G20 members expressed interest in adopting elements of India's DPI stack, recognizing its potential to bridge digital divides.
• This leadership position reinforces India's growing influence in shaping global digital governance and technological standards.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: DPI refers to shared digital systems for public good (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI).
G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
India has been a proponent of DPI for inclusive growth.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for developing economies.
Analyze how India's success with Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker positions it as a leader in global digital governance.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in promoting DPI adoption globally, especially in terms of data privacy and interoperability.
Examine the role of G20 platforms in fostering international cooperation for digital transformation.
articles.imageDescription: A world map overlaid with digital network lines and icons of digital services like payments and identity, symbolizing global DPI adoption.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processes billions of transactions monthly, making it one of the world's most successful digital payment systems.

articles.headline: Review Highlights Progress and Challenges in National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review meeting assessed the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states and Union Territories.
• The review noted significant strides in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and early childhood care and education initiatives.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, faculty recruitment, and ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources, especially in remote areas.
• The Ministry of Education reiterated its commitment to addressing these bottlenecks through increased funding and collaborative efforts with state governments.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic development and critical thinking.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
Focuses on 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Promotes vocational education from Class 6 onwards.
Aims for universal access to quality education.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key objectives and transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020.
Analyze the major successes and persistent challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020 across different educational levels.
Evaluate the role of technology and digital learning in achieving the goals of NEP 2020, particularly in remote and underserved areas.
Examine how NEP 2020 addresses the skill gap and prepares India's youth for the demands of the 21st-century workforce.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning activities, symbolizing the future of education.
articles.trivia: India's first National Policy on Education was formulated in 1968.

articles.headline: Indian Pharmaceutical Sector Strengthens Global Supply Chain Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India's pharmaceutical sector is increasingly playing a crucial role in strengthening global drug supply chain resilience, especially for essential medicines.
• The industry has ramped up production capabilities for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) and key starting materials, reducing reliance on single-source countries.
• Government initiatives, including Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes, have encouraged domestic manufacturing and innovation in critical drug segments.
• India's robust R&D infrastructure and cost-effective manufacturing capabilities make it a reliable partner in global health security.
• This strategic emphasis not only ensures domestic drug security but also positions India as a 'pharmacy of the world' with enhanced self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: India is known as the 'pharmacy of the world' for generic drugs.
PLI schemes are introduced to boost domestic manufacturing.
APIs are crucial raw materials for drug production.
Focus on reducing dependence on imports for critical drug components.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of a robust domestic pharmaceutical industry for India's health security and economic growth.
Analyze how government policies like PLI schemes are strengthening India's position in the global drug supply chain.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming self-reliant in Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs).
Examine India's role in global health security and its contributions to affordable medicine access worldwide.
articles.imageDescription: Pharmaceutical laboratory with researchers working on drug development, symbolizing innovation and production.
articles.trivia: India supplies over 50% of the global demand for various vaccines and 25% of all medicines in the UK.

highlights: Government launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting export hub status by 2030.
Supreme Court upholds key provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, balancing privacy and national security.
India gains strong African Union support for permanent membership in a reformed UN Security Council.
ISRO successfully tests crucial component for Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission, advancing towards launch.
RBI maintains status quo on policy rates amidst global economic uncertainties, prioritizing price stability and growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. Its primary aim is to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030.
2. The mission includes Production Linked Incentives (PLI) for electrolyzer manufacturing but not for green hydrogen production itself.
3. The mission is crucial for India's energy security and decarbonization goals.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect: The mission includes PLI for both electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production. Statement 3 is correct: It is vital for India's energy security and decarbonization goals.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for recommending the Minimum Support Prices (MSPs) for various crops in India?
options: (c) Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
(d) National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) is an attached office of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare that recommends MSPs for various crops based on comprehensive cost analysis and other factors.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM):
1. It aims to create a seamless online platform for patients to access their health records.
2. The mission primarily focuses on integrating only public healthcare providers, excluding private ones.
3. Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) is a unique health ID for citizens under this mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: ABDM aims to create a seamless online platform for patients to access health records and other services. Statement 2 is incorrect: The mission integrates both public and private healthcare providers. Statement 3 is correct: ABHA is a unique health ID for citizens under ABDM.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following countries are members of the QUAD (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue)?
1. India
2. Japan
3. Australia
4. South Korea
5. United States
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (QUAD) is a strategic forum comprising four countries: India, Japan, Australia, and the United States. South Korea is not a member of QUAD.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
2. It recommends a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure for school education.
3. It aims to integrate vocational education from Class 1 onwards.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986. Statement 2 is correct: It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure. Statement 3 is incorrect: NEP 2020 aims to integrate vocational education from Class 6 onwards, not Class 1.

mainsMocks.question: Critically examine the significance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving India's energy security and climate targets. Discuss the challenges involved in establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen production.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Discuss its significance for energy security (reduced import dependence) and climate targets (decarbonization, net-zero). Analyze the challenges such as high production costs, need for massive renewable energy capacity, infrastructure for storage and transport, R&D gaps, and international competition. Conclude with policy recommendations.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the concept of cooperative federalism in India and analyze how NITI Aayog contributes to fostering it. Illustrate with examples of successful Centre-state collaborations in implementing flagship schemes.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure; NITI Aayog.
mainsMocks.approach: Define cooperative federalism as a principle of Centre-state collaboration. Explain NITI Aayog's role as a think tank and facilitator, promoting policy dialogue, sharing best practices, and monitoring scheme implementation. Provide examples like Swachh Bharat Mission, Ayushman Bharat, or Jal Jeevan Mission where Centre-state cooperation has been vital for success. Conclude by highlighting its importance for inclusive national development.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, for individual privacy and national security. What are the key challenges in balancing these two aspects in the digital age?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Right to Privacy; Cybersecurity.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by stating the context of the DPDP Act and the Supreme Court's upholding of its provisions. Discuss the implications for individual privacy (rights of data principals, data fiduciaries' obligations) and national security (state's access to data under specific conditions). Elaborate on challenges like defining reasonable restrictions, preventing misuse of state power, ensuring judicial oversight, technological complexities, and public trust. Conclude with the need for robust safeguards and continuous review.

quickBytes.text: The Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the world's largest solar power plants.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) was established on April 1, 1935, under the RBI Act, 1934.
India's first astronaut, Rakesh Sharma, flew aboard a Soviet Soyuz T-11 mission in 1984.
The G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocate for permanent membership in the UNSC.
The SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) doctrine guides India's maritime engagements.
The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) recommends Minimum Support Prices (MSPs).
UNESCO designated the Galapagos Islands as the first World Heritage Site in 1978.
The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
India is the world's largest producer of generic medicines, earning it the title 'pharmacy of the world'.
The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).

GS1.headline: UNESCO Recognizes Indian Cultural Landscape as New World Heritage Site
GS1.explainer:
• UNESCO has officially inscribed a significant Indian cultural landscape onto its prestigious World Heritage List, marking a major recognition of India's rich heritage.
• The newly recognized site encompasses a unique blend of natural beauty, historical monuments, and living cultural traditions, reflecting centuries of human-environment interaction.
• This designation is expected to boost cultural tourism, create local employment opportunities, and enhance international awareness of India's diverse heritage.
• The government and local communities have committed to implementing robust conservation and management plans to preserve the site's outstanding universal value.
• India now boasts an even greater number of UNESCO World Heritage Sites, solidifying its position as a global cultural destination.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO World Heritage Sites are designated for their outstanding universal value.
Cultural landscapes are a category of World Heritage Sites.
India has numerous UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
UNESCO is a specialized agency of the United Nations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO World Heritage status for the conservation and promotion of India's cultural and natural heritage.
Analyze the challenges involved in managing and preserving cultural landscapes while balancing tourism and local community needs.
Evaluate the economic and social benefits that accrue to local communities from a World Heritage designation.
Examine how such recognition contributes to India's soft power and global cultural diplomacy.
GS1.imageDescription: An aerial view of a scenic Indian landscape featuring ancient ruins, traditional settlements, and natural formations.
GS1.trivia: The first site to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List was the Galapagos Islands in 1978.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Art & Culture
prelimsSyllabus.topic: UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India

GS2.headline: Former MP and Rajasthan MLA Nand Lal Meena Passes Away at 79
GS2.explainer:
• Veteran politician Nand Lal Meena, who served as a Member of Parliament and multiple terms as a Member of Legislative Assembly in Rajasthan, passed away at the age of 79.
• Meena represented the Salumber constituency in the Lok Sabha from 1989 to 1991.
• He was a prominent figure in Rajasthan politics, having been elected as an MLA from the Pratapgarh constituency for several terms between 1998 and 2013.
• Known for his focus on tribal welfare and regional development, he contributed significantly to public life in his home state.
• His demise marks the end of an era for a dedicated public servant from Rajasthan.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Nand Lal Meena was a former MP (1989-1991) from Salumber.
Served as Rajasthan MLA (1998-2013) from Pratapgarh.
Associated with tribal welfare in Rajasthan.
GS2.imageDescription: Black and white photo of a senior Indian politician, Nand Lal Meena.
GS2.trivia: The first Lok Sabha elections in independent India were held from 1951-52.

GS2.headline: India Gains Momentum for UNSC Reform with Strong African Union Support
GS2.explainer:
• India's long-standing demand for permanent membership in a reformed UN Security Council (UNSC) receives significant backing from the African Union (AU).
• During the ongoing UN General Assembly session, several AU member states reiterated their support for expanding the UNSC to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
• India has consistently advocated for comprehensive UNSC reforms, emphasizing equitable representation for developing nations, including from Africa and Latin America.
• This renewed support comes amidst growing calls for a more inclusive and effective global governance architecture capable of addressing 21st-century challenges.
• The G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) continue to coordinate efforts to push for reforms.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: India is a member of the G4 group advocating for UNSC reform.
African Union has expressed support for expanding UNSC permanent membership.
UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
mainsPoints: Analyze the arguments for and against India's permanent membership in the UNSC.
Discuss the significance of African Union's support for India's UNSC aspirations.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving comprehensive UN Security Council reforms.
Examine how a reformed UNSC could better address global challenges.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations building in New York with flags of member countries, symbolizing international diplomacy.
GS2.trivia: The UN Security Council held its first session on January 17, 1946.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling clarified that certain provisions, particularly those concerning the balance between individual privacy and national security/public order, are permissible under reasonable restrictions.
• While acknowledging concerns raised by petitioners regarding potential surveillance, the Court emphasized the necessity of a robust legal framework for data protection in the digital age.
• The judgment is expected to provide much-needed clarity for technology companies and data fiduciaries operating in India.
• This decision reinforces India's commitment to protecting citizen data while also allowing the state to maintain security and public order, setting a precedent for future digital governance.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Right to Privacy
Cybersecurity
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act, 2023 is India's primary data protection law.
Supreme Court decision relates to constitutional validity.
Balances privacy with national security/public order.
Puttaswamy judgment established Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the DPDP Act for individual privacy and state surveillance.
Discuss the challenges in balancing the right to privacy with concerns of national security and public order in the digital age.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the DPDP Act in protecting citizen data and fostering a responsible digital economy.
Examine the global trends in data protection laws and India's position within this landscape.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade, representing legal authority and justice.
GS2.trivia: Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) and Anr. vs Union Of India And Ors. (2017) is the landmark case that declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Digital Governance

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Reach, Integrates More Healthcare Providers
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has announced a significant expansion, bringing a greater number of public and private healthcare providers under its digital health ecosystem.
• This expansion aims to create a seamless online platform for patients to access their health records, consult doctors, and avail other services.
• The integration includes more hospitals, clinics, diagnostic labs, and pharmacies, enhancing the interoperability of health data across the country.
• A key feature is the emphasis on creating unique Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA) for citizens, acting as a digital repository for their medical history.
• The mission is pivotal in achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare delivery efficiency through digital means, aligning with the National Health Policy 2017.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem in India.
ABHA is a unique health ID for citizens.
National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
Focus on integrating public and private healthcare providers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in transforming India's healthcare landscape.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a nationwide digital health ecosystem, particularly concerning data privacy and digital literacy.
Evaluate how ABDM can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare access in rural areas.
Examine the ethical considerations and safeguards required for a robust digital health platform.
GS2.imageDescription: A digital interface displaying patient health records and a doctor interacting with a tablet, symbolizing digital healthcare.
GS2.trivia: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

GS2.headline: India-Australia Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Critical Minerals and Green Energy
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Australia Strategic Dialogue concluded with a strong emphasis on enhancing cooperation in critical minerals and green energy technologies.
• Both nations committed to developing resilient supply chains for critical minerals essential for advanced manufacturing and renewable energy sectors.
• Discussions also covered joint research and development initiatives in solar, hydrogen, and battery technologies, leveraging their respective strengths.
• The dialogue reinforced the comprehensive strategic partnership, aiming to diversify trade and investment beyond traditional sectors.
• This collaboration is vital for both countries' energy transition goals and for securing key resources in a geopolitically volatile world.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Australia is rich in critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements.
India-Australia relations elevated to Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2020.
Critical minerals are essential for electric vehicles, electronics, and renewable energy.
Both countries are part of the QUAD grouping.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth and energy transition.
Analyze how India-Australia cooperation in critical minerals and green energy can bolster supply chain resilience and reduce global dependencies.
Evaluate the broader implications of this partnership for the Indo-Pacific region and global energy security.
Examine the potential for joint R&D and investment in advanced energy technologies between the two nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Australia against a backdrop of wind turbines and mining operations, symbolizing energy and resources collaboration.
GS2.trivia: Australia is the world's largest producer of lithium, a key component in EV batteries.

GS2.headline: NITI Aayog Report Highlights Successes in Cooperative Federalism for Flagship Schemes
GS2.explainer:
• A recent report by NITI Aayog showcased several successful instances of cooperative federalism in action, particularly in the implementation of national flagship schemes.
• The report analyzed how collaborative efforts between the Centre and various states led to efficient delivery of services and achievement of development targets.
• Case studies included Swachh Bharat Mission, Ayushman Bharat, and Jal Jeevan Mission, highlighting best practices and innovative approaches adopted by states.
• NITI Aayog emphasized its role as a facilitator and think tank, promoting policy dialogue and knowledge sharing between different levels of government.
• This reinforces the importance of a 'Team India' approach to national development, where states are active partners in policy formulation and implementation.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
NITI Aayog
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015.
NITI Aayog acts as a think tank and promotes cooperative federalism.
Swachh Bharat Mission, Ayushman Bharat, Jal Jeevan Mission are flagship schemes.
Cooperative federalism emphasizes collaboration between Centre and States.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of cooperative federalism and its significance in India's governance structure.
Analyze the role of NITI Aayog in fostering cooperative federalism and improving policy implementation.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for Centre-state collaboration in achieving national development goals.
Examine how successful implementation of flagship schemes can be attributed to cooperative federalism.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected gears, symbolizing the collaborative functioning of central and state governments.
GS2.trivia: The term 'cooperative federalism' was first used in India by Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru.

GS2.headline: India Continues to Champion Digital Public Infrastructure at G20 Platforms
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to promoting Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) as a key enabler for inclusive growth and sustainable development at recent G20 meetings.
• Building on its successful domestic models like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, India advocated for global cooperation in developing open, interoperable, and secure DPIs.
• The focus was on leveraging DPI for financial inclusion, digital payments, identity verification, and efficient public service delivery in developing nations.
• Several G20 members expressed interest in adopting elements of India's DPI stack, recognizing its potential to bridge digital divides.
• This leadership position reinforces India's growing influence in shaping global digital governance and technological standards.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: DPI refers to shared digital systems for public good (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI).
G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
India has been a proponent of DPI for inclusive growth.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for developing economies.
Analyze how India's success with Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker positions it as a leader in global digital governance.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in promoting DPI adoption globally, especially in terms of data privacy and interoperability.
Examine the role of G20 platforms in fostering international cooperation for digital transformation.
GS2.imageDescription: A world map overlaid with digital network lines and icons of digital services like payments and identity, symbolizing global DPI adoption.
GS2.trivia: India's UPI system processes billions of transactions monthly, making it one of the world's most successful digital payment systems.

GS2.headline: Review Highlights Progress and Challenges in National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive review meeting assessed the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states and Union Territories.
• The review noted significant strides in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and early childhood care and education initiatives.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, faculty recruitment, and ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources, especially in remote areas.
• The Ministry of Education reiterated its commitment to addressing these bottlenecks through increased funding and collaborative efforts with state governments.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic development and critical thinking.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
Focuses on 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Promotes vocational education from Class 6 onwards.
Aims for universal access to quality education.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key objectives and transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020.
Analyze the major successes and persistent challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020 across different educational levels.
Evaluate the role of technology and digital learning in achieving the goals of NEP 2020, particularly in remote and underserved areas.
Examine how NEP 2020 addresses the skill gap and prepares India's youth for the demands of the 21st-century workforce.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning activities, symbolizing the future of education.
GS2.trivia: India's first National Policy on Education was formulated in 1968.

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Green Hydrogen Mission - Phase II, Targets Export Hub Status
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government unveils the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and fostering an export-oriented ecosystem.
• New incentives include production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production, along with land allocation and infrastructure development.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• Focus areas include research and development in cost-reduction technologies, skill development, and strategic international partnerships for market access.
• This phase is crucial for India's energy security, decarbonization goals, and creating green jobs across the value chain.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Phase II targets India as a global green hydrogen export hub by 2030.
PLI scheme for electrolyzers and green hydrogen production is a key component.
Aims to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve climate targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic and environmental significance of India's Green Hydrogen Mission.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global green hydrogen hub.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in accelerating the green hydrogen economy.
Examine the potential impact on India's energy security and geopolitical standing.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with renewable energy sources like solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework for Sustainable Urban Development Emphasizes Green Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveils a comprehensive policy framework aimed at promoting sustainable urban development across Indian cities.
• Key focus areas include integrating green infrastructure, promoting waste-to-energy projects, and enhancing urban resilience to climate change.
• The policy mandates the adoption of green building codes, increased public transport usage, and decentralized waste management systems.
• It also emphasizes citizen participation in urban planning and leveraging technology for smart governance.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitments under the Paris Agreement and Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
GS3.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: New policy framework by Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Focus on green infrastructure and waste-to-energy in urban areas.
Aims to achieve SDG 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities.
Promotes green building codes and decentralized waste management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of rapid urbanization in India and how sustainable urban development policies can address them.
Evaluate the role of green infrastructure and waste-to-energy projects in achieving urban sustainability.
Critically analyze the effectiveness of current urban planning models in India and suggest improvements.
Examine the potential of citizen participation and technology in fostering smart and sustainable cities.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern cityscape with green spaces, solar panels on rooftops, and efficient public transport, depicting sustainable urban living.
GS3.trivia: Around 35% of India's population lives in urban areas, a number projected to increase significantly by 2050.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Crucial Component for Gaganyaan Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful testing of a critical component for the Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission.
• This test involved the integrated performance validation of the crew escape system's propulsion elements under simulated flight conditions.
• The successful validation brings ISRO closer to its ambitious goal of sending Indian astronauts into space aboard an indigenous spacecraft.
• Future tests will focus on full-scale abort missions and further integration of various modules.
• The Gaganyaan mission aims to demonstrate India's capability for human spaceflight by launching a crew of three into a low earth orbit for a mission duration of three days.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
ISRO is the agency responsible for Gaganyaan.
Crew escape system is a vital safety component for manned missions.
Mission aims to send 3 astronauts to LEO for 3 days.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance and scientific benefits of India's Gaganyaan mission.
Analyze the technological challenges and advancements required for human spaceflight missions.
Evaluate the potential spin-off technologies and economic benefits from space missions like Gaganyaan.
Examine India's growing role in global space exploration and cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: An artist's impression of the Gaganyaan spacecraft module with the Indian flag, orbiting Earth.
GS3.trivia: India's first astronaut, Rakesh Sharma, flew aboard a Soviet Soyuz T-11 mission in 1984.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision comes amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and fluctuating commodity prices, which pose risks to domestic inflation.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to maintaining price stability while supporting sustainable economic growth.
• Analysts suggest that the RBI is adopting a cautious 'wait and watch' approach, assessing the impact of previous rate hikes and global developments before further adjustments.
• The central bank reiterated its focus on liquidity management to ensure financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines policy rates in India.
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability.
Current inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain status quo on policy rates.
Analyze the impact of global economic headwinds on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the RBI's current monetary policy stance in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Examine the challenges faced by the MPC in achieving its dual mandate.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: India Bolsters Maritime Security Cooperation in Indo-Pacific
GS3.explainer:
• India has significantly enhanced its maritime security cooperation with key partners in the Indo-Pacific region through a series of multilateral exercises and bilateral agreements.
• The focus is on strengthening interoperability, information sharing, and collective response capabilities to address traditional and non-traditional security threats.
• Recent joint naval exercises with QUAD partners (US, Japan, Australia) and ASEAN countries underscore India's commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• This strategic push aims to counter growing challenges such as piracy, illegal fishing, and maritime terrorism, while also ensuring freedom of navigation.
• India's vision for SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) guides its engagements, emphasizing capacity building and collaborative security frameworks.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: SAGAR doctrine relates to India's vision for security and growth in the Indian Ocean Region.
QUAD members are India, USA, Japan, Australia.
Malabar exercise involves QUAD nations.
Indo-Pacific is a key geopolitical region for India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving maritime security challenges in the Indo-Pacific region and India's strategic response.
Analyze the significance of India's partnerships with QUAD and ASEAN countries in enhancing regional maritime security.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the SAGAR doctrine in promoting a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Examine the implications of increased maritime cooperation for India's geopolitical influence and economic interests.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian Navy warships sailing in formation with other international naval vessels during a joint exercise in the open sea.
GS3.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's five oceans.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New Support Prices for Rabi Crops, Focus on Farmer Income
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government announced the Minimum Support Prices (MSPs) for the upcoming Rabi marketing season, with significant hikes for key crops.
• The move is aimed at ensuring remunerative prices for farmers, encouraging crop diversification, and bolstering agricultural productivity.
• Wheat, barley, gram, masur (lentil), rapeseed & mustard, and safflower are among the crops for which MSPs have been increased.
• The government highlighted its commitment to implementing the Swaminathan Committee recommendations, ensuring MSPs are at least 1.5 times the cost of production.
• This policy decision is expected to have a positive impact on farmer incomes and address concerns regarding agricultural distress, while also managing food inflation.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security.
keyTerms: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsPoints: MSP is announced by the government for certain crops to protect farmers from price fluctuations.
Rabi crops are sown in winter and harvested in spring (e.g., wheat, barley, gram).
CACP recommends MSPs.
Swaminathan Committee recommended MSP at 1.5 times the cost of production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and implications of the government's MSP policy for Rabi crops on farmer welfare and agricultural economy.
Analyze the challenges associated with the implementation of MSP, including procurement, storage, and market distortions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of MSP in ensuring remunerative prices and promoting crop diversification.
Examine alternative mechanisms to support farmer income and address agricultural distress in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer working in a lush green wheat field during the Rabi season, symbolizing agricultural productivity.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of wheat, after China.

GS3.headline: India Records Significant Solar Energy Capacity Addition in Q3 2025
GS3.explainer:
• India has achieved a new milestone in renewable energy, adding a record amount of solar energy capacity in the third quarter of 2025.
• This surge is attributed to supportive government policies, declining solar panel costs, and increased private sector investment.
• The aggressive expansion is crucial for India to meet its ambitious target of 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• States like Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Karnataka continue to lead in solar power generation, attracting major domestic and international players.
• The rapid growth in solar energy contributes significantly to reducing India's carbon footprint and enhancing energy independence.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: India's target is 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Solar energy is a major component of India's renewable energy mix.
Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Karnataka are leading states in solar power.
International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to India's rapid growth in solar energy capacity.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating large-scale solar power into India's national grid.
Evaluate the role of solar energy in achieving India's climate targets and enhancing energy security.
Examine the socio-economic benefits of expanding renewable energy infrastructure, including job creation and rural electrification.
GS3.imageDescription: Vast solar panel arrays stretching across a desert landscape under a clear sky, generating clean energy.
GS3.trivia: Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the world's largest solar power plants.

GS3.headline: DRDO Successfully Tests Advanced Indigenous Drone System
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) announced the successful flight testing of a new advanced indigenous drone system.
• This Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) is designed for enhanced surveillance, reconnaissance, and logistics support capabilities for the Indian armed forces.
• The test demonstrated its long endurance, advanced navigation systems, and ability to operate in diverse terrains and weather conditions.
• This development is a significant step towards 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) in defense technology, reducing reliance on foreign imports.
