• The drone system is expected to augment India's border security and tactical operational capabilities significantly.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Defence Technology
prelimsPoints: DRDO is India's premier defense research agency.
UAVs are increasingly used for surveillance and logistics.
Atmanirbhar Bharat aims for self-reliance in defense manufacturing.
Drones are crucial for modern warfare and border management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous drone technology for India's defense capabilities and national security.
Analyze the role of DRDO in achieving self-reliance in critical defense technologies.
Evaluate the ethical and operational challenges associated with the increasing use of UAVs in military operations.
Examine how advanced drone systems can enhance border management and counter-insurgency operations.
GS3.imageDescription: A sleek, modern drone flying over a mountainous border region, performing surveillance.
GS3.trivia: The first military drone, the 'Ruston Proctor Aerial Target,' was developed during World War I.

GS3.headline: New Initiative Launched to Boost Climate-Resilient Agriculture Practices
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, in collaboration with ICAR, launched a new national initiative to promote climate-resilient agriculture.
• The program focuses on developing and disseminating climate-smart farming practices, drought-resistant crop varieties, and efficient water management techniques.
• It aims to help Indian farmers adapt to the adverse impacts of climate change, such as erratic rainfall, extreme temperatures, and increased pest outbreaks.
• Training and capacity building for farmers, along with financial incentives for adopting sustainable practices, are key components of the initiative.
• This move is crucial for ensuring food security and sustaining agricultural livelihoods in the face of a changing climate.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: ICAR is the apex body for coordinating, guiding and managing research and education in agriculture.
Climate-resilient agriculture aims to adapt to and mitigate climate change impacts.
Focus on drought-resistant varieties and efficient water use.
Crucial for India's food security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and the need for climate-resilient practices.
Analyze the components of the new initiative for climate-resilient agriculture and its potential impact on farmer livelihoods.
Evaluate the role of research and technology in developing climate-smart farming solutions.
Examine how government policies and farmer participation can accelerate the adoption of sustainable agricultural practices.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting healthy, green crops in a field, with advanced irrigation systems, demonstrating climate-resilient farming.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the most climate-vulnerable countries, with agriculture being particularly susceptible to climate change impacts.

GS3.headline: Indian Pharmaceutical Sector Strengthens Global Supply Chain Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• India's pharmaceutical sector is increasingly playing a crucial role in strengthening global drug supply chain resilience, especially for essential medicines.
• The industry has ramped up production capabilities for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) and key starting materials, reducing reliance on single-source countries.
• Government initiatives, including Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes, have encouraged domestic manufacturing and innovation in critical drug segments.
• India's robust R&D infrastructure and cost-effective manufacturing capabilities make it a reliable partner in global health security.
• This strategic emphasis not only ensures domestic drug security but also positions India as a 'pharmacy of the world' with enhanced self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: India is known as the 'pharmacy of the world' for generic drugs.
PLI schemes are introduced to boost domestic manufacturing.
APIs are crucial raw materials for drug production.
Focus on reducing dependence on imports for critical drug components.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of a robust domestic pharmaceutical industry for India's health security and economic growth.
Analyze how government policies like PLI schemes are strengthening India's position in the global drug supply chain.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming self-reliant in Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs).
Examine India's role in global health security and its contributions to affordable medicine access worldwide.
GS3.imageDescription: Pharmaceutical laboratory with researchers working on drug development, symbolizing innovation and production.
GS3.trivia: India supplies over 50% of the global demand for various vaccines and 25% of all medicines in the UK.
